title: cotoneaster uva-ursi
intro: Cotoneaster uva-ursi, also known as bearberry, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its red or yellow berries and oval-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating urinary tract infections and kidney stones due to its high concentration of arbutin, a compound with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Cotoneaster uva-ursi include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve kidney function, and act as a natural diuretic. Herbal preparations of Cotoneaster uva-ursi can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to support urinary health and prevent the formation of kidney stones.
